behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System, because this failure is at the git CLI boundary (real rename detection + parser handoff), not a single pure function. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration is too low since mocked git output can hide real rename status formats (e.g., R100/path handling) and subprocess behavior; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text (no AI model, no agent pipeline). FINAL: System.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit `a.txt`, rename it with `git mv` to `b.txt`, run the CLI, and assert report shows 1 renamed, 0 added, 0 deleted for that change. This requires real git subprocess + filesystem state; a fake adapter could miss true rename-status behavior.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System, because binary-diff behavior depends on real git diff output and subprocess execution, where crashes often occur. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration with fixtures is insufficient since mocked text output can miss real binary markers/edge formatting; Agentic/Workflow rejected by behavior text (pure git/CLI behavior, no model output). FINAL: System.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, commit a binary file (e.g., random bytes), then modify/add binary files, run the CLI, assert non-error exit and binary files included in category counts. Uses real filesystem + git subprocess output to expose crash paths.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System, because the critical failure is handling real git command failures/exit codes in an unborn-HEAD repo. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration mocks can force success/failure but miss actual git stderr/return-code semantics; Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (no AI dependence). FINAL: System.
behaviors.test_description: `git init` a fresh repo with no commits, run the CLI, assert successful execution and an empty structured report (all zero/empty categories) instead of error. Real subprocess behavior is the failure boundary.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System, since correctness is explicitly against real repository history, requiring end-to-end interaction among git history, subprocess output, and report aggregation. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration fakes can validate aggregator math but can hide real history-query mismatches; Agentic/Workflow rejected by behavior text (no ML/LLM decision quality involved). FINAL: System.
behaviors.test_description: Script a real repo history containing known adds/deletes/renames/modifications across commits, run the CLI over that repo, and compare report counts to expected counts derived from the scripted ground truth. This catches real handoff issues from git history to category totals.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System, because staged vs unstaged correctness depends on real git index/worktree state transitions and subprocess queries. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration mocks usually normalize these states and can hide index-specific bugs; Agentic/Workflow disqualified by behavior text (no model call, no multi-agent chain). FINAL: System.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real repo and create identical file edits twice: once staged (`git add`) and once unstaged. Run the CLI in each state and assert byte-identical structured stdout. Requires real git index + working tree semantics.
